statement.p: If you add {0} to a certain positive number, then also subtract 1 from that same number, and multiply the results, you get {{\frac{3}{4}}}\text{.} What was the original number?

solution.p: Let x be the mystery number. We are told that \left(x+{0}\right)\left(x-1\right)={{\frac{3}{4}}}\text{.} So
Now we can apply the quadratic formula:
Both {-{\frac{1}{2}}} and {{\frac{3}{2}}} solve the equation, but we were asked to find a positive number. So the mystery number is {{\frac{3}{2}}}\text{.}
